Number Theory > Divisibility / Factorization > Prime numbers
prompt:
$N$ positive integer numbers are given, such that greatest common divisors of all nonempty sets of these numbers are pairwise distinct. Determine the smallest possible number of distinct prime divisors of the product of these $N$ numbers.
(OleksandrGolovanov)
